---
title: "A composable ecosystem for infectious disease modelling"
description: "Why we are building EpiAware, and where it is today."
author: "Sam Abbott"
date: "2026-07-10"
categories: [announcement]
---

Turing-based composability for infectious disease modelling.
This brings the case for composable modelling, made in the epiforecasts modelling workflow and composability grant, into Julia.

- **Aim**: interchangeable components that combine into joint models, propagate
  uncertainty, and make outbreak analysis workflows easier to build.
- **Approach**: multiple interoperable approaches — [composing
  distributions](/tutorials/composing-distributions.qmd) into event trees,
  [assembling a full model](/tutorials/assembled-model.qmd) from parts, and
  more as the ecosystem grows.
- **Alongside**: other efforts with the same goal —
  [DDSALineLists.jl](https://github.com/epirecipes/DDSALineLists.jl),
  [CategoricalOutbreaks.jl](https://github.com/epirecipes/CategoricalOutbreaks.jl),
  [EpiBranch.jl](https://epiforecasts.io/EpiBranch.jl/dev/).

## Where it is today

[CensoredDistributions.jl](/packages/censoreddistributions.qmd) is the most developed.
The joint-modelling and composition packages are in active development.
Each package's page says how settled it is.

## Get involved

Collaborators welcome — code, docs, examples, or a modelling problem.
See [Get involved](/get-involved.qmd), or start a conversation on the [epinowcast forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/).
